title: jinx
text: R package powering RLadies+ GitHub organization bot. Deployed GitHub App (jinx[bot]) via GitHub Actions workflows.

title: jinx
text: R package (TypeScript/Probot) — R-centric org, maintainability GitHub App identity via actions/create-github-app-token@v1 /jinx commands issue comments trigger commands.yml workflow Scheduled workflows handle recurring tasks (reports, sync, monitoring) 16 modules across 50 R source files, 24 workflows, 25 templates

title: jinx
text: workflows use actions/create-github-app-token@v1 repo secrets (variables): Always reference secrets.JINX_APP_ID, never vars.JINX_APP_ID.

title: jinx
text: Slack bridge runs Cloudflare Worker https://jinx.rladies.workers.dev.

title: jinx
text: Routes follow /<service>/<action> — never flat paths like /slack-interact /airtable-webhook. Slack-side endpoints run verifySlackSignature() router; /airtable/webhook uses x-airtable-secret header.
code: /slack/command      POST   Slash commands from Slack /slack/events       POST   Events API (app_mention, etc.) /slack/interact     POST   Block Kit button clicks from Slack /slack/install      GET    Start OAuth install flow /slack/oauth        GET    OAuth callback /airtable/webhook   POST   Form submissions from Airtable

title: jinx
text: Airtable webhook fires new invitee row → worker posts Block Kit card Approve / Deny buttons SLACK_COMMUNITY_INVITE_CHANNEL. Click Deny: Airtable record marked denied = true, card replaced denial note. Done. Click Approve: card replaced place manual-invite checklist (workspace menu → Invite people → paste email) single Mark invite sent button. Airtable change yet. organiser sends invite via Slack workspace settings, clicks Mark invite sent card. flip writes invited = true Airtable, replaces card final “✅ Invite sent @user — approved @approver” message. Airtable invited field must mean invite actually sent, approved. Don’t flip Approve — wait Mark invite sent click.

title: jinx
text: Every /airtable/webhook POST must include originating base table worker can write back right place. Airtable automation script responsible passing IDs: base_id table_id required. Use table ID (tbl…) rather table name — survives renames. record_id, base_id, table_id round-trip Block Kit button value approve/deny/mark-sent handlers can PATCH originating base.

title: jinx
text: Allowed bases discovered dynamically calling Airtable’s Meta API (GET /v0/meta/bases) AIRTABLE_API_KEY. Airtable PAT’s scope allowlist — base token can see accepted. Granting token broader access broadens Jinx accept; scope PAT invitee-flow bases . result cached AIRTABLE_BASES KV namespace key allowed_bases 1-hour TTL (worker/src/airtable-meta.js). Refresh cache miss; fail closed Meta API errors refresh — webhook returns HTTP 503. Bust cache early deleting key KV.

title: jinx
text: Slack workspace tokens managed via OAuth, stored Cloudflare KV (SLACK_TOKENS namespace) keyed team:<team_id>. Use getSlackToken(env, teamId) look tokens — never hardcode workspace-specific tokens. KV entry JSON written /slack/oauth callback successful install: manual seeding needed — install app workspace via /slack/install callback writes entry. revoke, delete team:<id> key KV. token fallback. getSlackToken(env, teamId) doesn’t find KV entry team:<teamId>, throws. Install via OAuth doesn’t work.

title: jinx
text: Jinx distribution-enabled Slack can OAuth RLadies+’s two workspaces (organisers + community), public app. handleSlackOAuthCallback runs isAllowedTeam(env, teamId) SLACK_ORGANIZER_TEAM_ID SLACK_COMMUNITY_TEAM_ID. team’s install attempt rejected HTTP 403 never reaches KV. add workspace: set team ID worker secret, redeploy, run /slack/install workspace.

title: jinx
text: code comments except workaround explanations tidyverse style, roxygen2 docs testthat describe/structure Use cli:: console output (cli_alert_*, cli_abort, cli_h2) Use cli::format_inline() string interpolation instead glue::glue() Use sprintf() technical strings (GraphQL queries, regex patterns) Keep paste() data operations (vector collapsing, auth headers) Internal functions get @keywords internal @noRd Hash-pipe (#|) chunk options vignettes

title: jinx
code: R/                    # 50 source files, organized by module tests/testthat/       # describe/it tests, 330+ passing inst/config/          # YAML configs (teams, review rules, labels, events, conferences, languages) inst/templates/       # Markdown templates with <KEY> placeholders inst/translations/    # i18n: en, es, pt, fr inst/schemas/         # JSON Schema validation (chapter, blog, directory, global-team, cfp, event) inst/commands/        # help.md for /jinx help .github/workflows/    # 24 workflows vignettes/            # getting-started.Rmd, workflows.Rmd

text: Templates inst/templates/ use <KEY> placeholders replaced render_template(). i18n module extends language-specific templates inst/translations/{lang}/ fall back English.

title: jinx
text: Run via zsh ensure PATH includes /opt/homebrew/bin (pandoc, gh).
code: devtools::test()    # 330+ tests devtools::check()   # must pass with 0 errors, 0 warnings

title: jinx
text: Worker JS vitest suite (R involved): vitest suite runs prereq infra-deploy-worker.yml, failing test blocks deploy. smoke test runs daily every successful deploy via infra-slack-smoke.yml, posts SLACK_HEALTHCHECK_CHANNEL failure.
code: cd worker && npm ci && npm test     # unit tests, no network cd worker && npm run smoke           # hits real Slack + deployed worker

title: Privacy Policy
text: Last updated: 2026-05-13 Jinx Slack app GitHub bot maintained volunteers RLadies+ organization. exists help RLadies+ run community operations — answering questions RLadies+ Guide, running organization commands inviting members, generating reports, reminding maintainers stale issues. document describes data Jinx receives, used, goes, request deletion.

title: Privacy Policy
text: Slack request received Cloudflare Worker operated RLadies+, verifies Slack’s request signature. Worker forwards command payload rladies/jinx GitHub repository repository_dispatch event. GitHub Actions workflow runs requested command jinx[bot] GitHub App posts result back Slack using response_url. command Slack user ran recorded GitHub Actions run log maintainers can audit Jinx . rladies/jinx repository public, means run logs publicly visible.

title: Privacy Policy
text: Cloudflare Worker receives mention. question text sent Cloudflare Workers AI embedding large language model generate answer grounded RLadies+ Guide website. answer (source links) posted back Slack thread. Worker store question answer outside Cloudflare’s standard observability logs.

title: Privacy Policy
text: Jinx maintains small Cloudflare KV store described table . store content messages, questions ask, answers gives, beyond standard observability logs .

title: Privacy Policy
text: GitHub — dispatch commands, run workflows, post replies via GitHub API. Cloudflare — Worker runtime, AI inference, Vectorize index run Cloudflare infrastructure. Slack — replies acknowledgements go back Slack via Web API. Jinx share data advertisers, analytics providers, third party listed . RLadies+ sell user data.

title: Privacy Policy
text: can ask maintainers : Remove Slack identifiers associated GitHub Actions run logs (redact relevant logs , possible, delete workflow run). Stop responding slash commands mentions (can also simply invoke Jinx — Jinx never receives data unless summon ). Provide list audit-trail entries reference Slack user ID. Send requests jinx@rladies.org open private contact Global Team member. aim respond within 30 days.

title: Privacy Policy
text: requests Slack verified using Slack’s signing secret processing. Communication Slack, GitHub, Cloudflare uses TLS. Secrets (GitHub App private key, Slack signing secret) stored Cloudflare Worker secrets GitHub Actions secrets, accessible deployment runtime. Bugs security issues can reported privately via contact email .

title: How Jinx is built
text: R package source truth: every workflow boots image pre-installed, worker exists put Slack-shaped inputs onto workflow trigger R code can answer .
code: +--------------------+      +-------------------------+ |  Slack workspace   |      |   GitHub (rladies/*)    | |                    |      |                         | |  /jinx ...         |      |  /jinx ... in a comment | |  @Jinx ...         |      |  PR opened, push, cron  | |  Block Kit clicks  |      |                         | +---------+----------+      +------------+------------+           |                              |           v                              v +---------------------+        +-----------------------+ |  Cloudflare Worker  | -----> |  GitHub Actions       | |  (worker/src/)      |  RPC   |  workflows (.github/) | |                     |        |                       | |  - OAuth + tokens   |        |  - jinx[bot] App auth | |  - signature verify |        |  - bot image w/ pkg   | |  - RAG (Vectorize)  |        |  - Rscript -e jinx::* | |  - Airtable webhook |        |                       | +---------------------+        +----------+------------+                                           |                                           v                                   +---------------+                                   |  R package    |                                   |  (this repo)  |                                   |               |                                   |  R/, inst/    |                                   +---------------+

title: How Jinx is built
text: bulk Jinx regular R package R/ exported functions grouped module — team management, chapters, directory, blog, announcements, reports, events, conferences, contributors, i18n, Slack, analytics, PR review. function reference lists everything group. Two things distinguish typical R package: Templates config live inst/. Issue-comment replies, onboarding checklists, command help, translations, team rules data files R functions read runtime. Editing template never requires code change. runs unattended. calls happen runner, someone’s session. Functions log cli, surface errors cli::cli_abort(), prefer returning structured data workflow can post back. can call exported function locally — gt_invite(), report_generate(), announce_post() — given right env vars. bot’s behaviour stays testable without spinning workflow.

title: How Jinx is built
text: Workflows .github/workflows/ runtime. share shape: Mint installation token actions/create-github-app-token using JINX_APP_ID + JINX_PRIVATE_KEY. token authenticates API calls gives bot jinx[bot] identity comments. Run inside ghcr.io/rladies/jinx-bot:latest R package dependencies already installed. image rebuilt infra-build-bot-image.yml runtime files change. Call package via Rscript -e 'jinx::...'. Workflows fall four naming buckets: Cross-repo PR review piece RLadies+ repos opt directly — call reusable-pr-review.yml workflow.

what: Cloudflare Worker — the Slack / Airtable front door
title: How Jinx is built
text: worker https://jinx.rladies.workers.dev lives worker/src/ piece Jinx run GitHub infrastructure. exists Slack Airtable need low-latency HTTPS endpoint Actions provide. Five things happen : OAuth token storage — slack-oauth.js runs install flow stashes per-workspace bot tokens Cloudflare KV (SLACK_TOKENS). allowlist check (organisers + community workspaces ) rejects install outside RLadies+. Signature verification — every Slack-side POST runs slack_signature_verify() slack-api.js business logic. Slash commands mentions — slash-command.js slack-events.js translate Slack payloads repository_dispatch events fire matching bot-* workflow, post workflow’s reply back via response_url. Slack sees normal slash-command response; GitHub sees normal workflow run. RAG answers — rag.js handles @Jinx-mention questions querying Cloudflare Vectorize index RLadies+ Guide website, using Workers AI answer. path stays entirely inside worker — GitHub round-trip. Airtable invite approval — airtable-invite.js receives webhook public invite form posts Block Kit cards organisers’ channel; slack-interact handles Approve / Deny / Mark-sent button clicks. worker deployed infra-deploy-worker.yml configured Wrangler secrets KV bindings, documented .github/AGENTS.md.

title: How Jinx is built
text: Two flows account almost everything Jinx . /jinx ... anywhere R code answers Slack GitHub. worker’s job make Slack request look like workflow trigger. @Jinx ... mention Mentions never leave worker — RAG fast enough round-tripping Actions wasteful.
code: Slack /jinx ...                Issue-comment /jinx ...        |                                |        v                                v    Worker                           bot-commands.yml   (verify, dispatch)                   (issue_comment)        |                                |        +--------> bot-commands.yml <----+                        |                        v                 jinx::cmd_parse()                 jinx::cmd_execute()                        |                        v               reply (Slack response_url                      or gh::gh comment) Slack @Jinx ...        |        v    Worker        |        v    rag.js  -- Vectorize query --> embedded chunks        |        v    Workers AI (Llama 3.1)        |        v    reply in-thread (chat.postMessage)

title: How Jinx is built
text: change spans surfaces, prefer one PR per surface unless land together — bot image rebuild + worker deploy run separate workflows roll independently.

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: jinx standard R package. GitHub Actions workflows call exported R functions. gh package handles GitHub API authentication via GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable. using GitHub App token (via actions/create-github-app-token), API calls comments appear jinx[bot].
code: Issue comment (/jinx invite ...)   -> issue_comment workflow triggers     -> installs jinx R package       -> calls jinx::cmd_parse() + jinx::cmd_execute()         -> gh::gh() API calls as jinx[bot]           -> reply comment posted

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: Go Settings > Developer settings > GitHub Apps > New org Name: jinx Repository: Issues (R/W), Pull Requests (R/W), Contents (Read) Organization: Members (R/W), Administration (Read) Events: issue_comment, issues, pull_request Install RLadies+ org

title: The Jinx Slack app
text: Clicking button opens standard Slack OAuth flow. asked workspace install Jinx permissions grant. Approving stores Jinx’s bot token workspace Cloudflare KV; revoking later (Settings & administration → Manage apps) drops token automatically. Jinx installs two RLadies+ workspaces (organisers community). try install elsewhere OAuth callback reject install nothing written. complete app configuration open source rladies/jinx want fork copy.

title: The Jinx Slack app
text: Type /jinx help channel see full command list. Common ones: /jinx invite @user <team> — invite someone RLadies+ GitHub org one teams. /jinx report weekly — generate activity report org. /jinx slack-invite <email> — post manual-invite checklist organiser action (see Approving Slack invites ). /jinx blog-add <url> — auto-create blog entry PR website URL. full reference, kept sync package, inst/commands/help.md.

title: The Jinx Slack app
text: Mention @Jinx channel member ask question. Jinx searches RLadies+ Guide RLadies+ website relevant content, replies -thread sourced links.
code: @Jinx how do I start a new chapter?

title: The Jinx Slack app
text: Anyone can request join RLadies+ community Slack via public Airtable form. new request comes , Jinx posts Block Kit card organisers’ invite-approval channel Approve / Deny buttons. Deny: marks Airtable record denied replaces card denial note. Done. Approve: replaces card checklist (workspace menu → Invite people RLadies+ → paste email) Mark invite sent button. organiser still actual invite manually — Slack give community apps API . invite sent, Mark invite sent button flips Airtable record’s invited flag replaces card final receipt. Airtable invited field means invite actually sent, just approved. Don’t flip Mark invite sent click.

title: The Jinx Slack app
text: Jinx requests following Slack scopes install : Jinx read messages isn’t directly mentioned . keeps database users messages. Per-workspace bot tokens stored Cloudflare KV; everything else ephemeral. See PRIVACY.md full data-handling policy.

title: The Jinx Slack app
text: /jinx ... returns “Jinx runs RLadies+ organisers community workspaces”: workspace ’re typing isn’t allowlist. Check team id /team-id workspace admin contact RLadies+ global team think added. OAuth install completes /jinx help still says thing: new team id needs added worker’s SLACK_ORGANIZER_TEAM_ID SLACK_COMMUNITY_TEAM_ID var, worker redeployed. Open issue https://github.com/rladies/jinx/issues. Jinx doesn’t respond @-mentions private channel: Jinx needs invited channel first (/invite @Jinx).

title: RLadies+ GitHub Organisation Management
text: RLadies+ operations bot — R package runs via GitHub Actions jinx[bot], Cloudflare Worker bridging Slack machinery. Jinx helps organiser onboarding, directory PR review, chapter monitoring, announcements, reports, event sync, translation checks, Slack invites RLadies+ community.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: Initial release. jinx automates organisational workflows RLadies+ GitHub org: chapter onboarding health, directory validation, blog announcement publishing, PR review, Airtable sync, Slack, analytics, events, CFP coordination, i18n. Exported functions follow <module>_<verb>[_<object>] schema group cleanly autocomplete. Short prefixes used established acronyms (cmd_, gt_, li_, cfp_, gha_, i18n_). Triggered via /jinx issue comments (cmd_parse() / cmd_execute()) 24 GitHub Actions workflows. Ships pkgdown site, Getting Started Workflow Reference vignettes, starter translations Spanish, Portuguese, French.
